text: <par>Written Burmese <latinform>-y</latinform> is a learned folk etymology (cf. Sanskrit).</par>
<par>Question mark in WSC indicates uncertainty over reconstructed form.</par>
<par>forms from GSTC 1006e-f correspond to PKB-STC p. 182, n.</par>
<par>HPTB, p. 173 gives a wrong GSR number "74a".</par>

<par>STC assigns the Chinese dental-initialled form to this etymon, assuming prefix-preemption (<reconstruction>*d-bu</reconstruction> > <reconstruction>*d-wu</reconstruction> > <reconstruction>*du</reconstruction>), but another possible prototype is <xref ref="1223">#1223</xref>, here.</par>
<par>Is source GSR or ACST? Was labeled ACST. Should ACST be re-labeled GSR?</par>
<par>Should the OC form be <latinform>gʻwɑ̂n</latinform> (<latinform>w</latinform> and <latinform>a</latinform> swapped)? cf. HPTB p.423</par>
<par>(and rel. to O Chin SPLEEN #29742, p. 24)?)</par>
